item.title: Building a Carshare
item.description:
Do you want a shared car, or do you want to share your car?

I recently re-structured a car sharing group, together with 9 other people and two vehicles. I can now drive a car as much as I like for less than I would have otherwise pay to own …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: A Bank.Green Recap
item.description:
Being good at doing good

Say you've just graduated college, bright, starry-eyed, and passionate about doing good in the world. Your first move may be to join a mission-driven organization where you'll spend a few years working on issues you care about, maybe visiting an orphanage or two, maybe surviving …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Structure and Governance for Intentional Communities
item.description:
I was recently involved in designing a governance structure for the intentional ecological community in which I live. This is an overview of that structure followed by a translated and heavily edited version of the structure itself.

Edit from June 2026, roughly 3 years after publication: This structure continues to …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: parcelLab
item.description: Managed large-scale data ingestion from customers and carriers at parcelLab.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Defunding Putins War
item.description: Are you feeling upset but helpless as Ukraine’s war rumbles on? It might not be realistic to suddenly stop buying gas and you’re probably not in a position to pick up a gun and join the fight. And as you’ve found your way here, you were probably …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Learning Dutch the Slow Way
item.description:
The world's full of pithy headlines: How to stop procrastinating. "Learn to code in 15 minutes." "7 Emails you should send every week." "How to Learn any Language in 3 Months."

They're needy. They overpromise. And they underdeliver. They always do.

This is post about learning Dutch the slow way …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: A Conquistador, the Climate Crisis, and the Finances of Atrocity
item.description:
Content warning: violence, sexual assault

Hernán Cortés' greed knew no bounds. Even after he and his 600 soldiers landed in present-day Mexico, toppled the Aztec Empire, murdered some 100,000 people, raped and enslaved uncounted others, and looted its precious stones, gold, and silver, he wanted more.

He went on …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Closing The BikePoints Project
item.description: "BikePoint" wayfinding is an innovative system of wayfinding in widespread use in Western Europe, but practically unknown and unheard of in the United States. A BikePoint wayfinding system is made of many "bike points," which are of numbered intersections connected by well-labeled bike-friendly routes. They simplify bicycle navigation while routing …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: October Update
item.description: As chill winds descend from the north and leaves turn to yellow and red, fall, and crunch underfoot, I wish we could huddle together, sharing hot drinks and thoughts, and feeling comfort in one another’s presence. Instead, we congregate over Zoom calls and emails, seeking connectedness in the harsh …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: A Dutch Import We Can All Love: Bike Points
item.description:
by Albert Carter and Alex Crompton.

Update, November 4, 2020: a BikePoints website is now live.

Update, October 15, 2020: The City of Cambridge, MA is showing some interest in this, since it fits well into the Cambridge 2020 Bicycle Plan. If you're also interested in this, please encourage them …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: 3. Amplifying Corporate Dissent: A Tactic to Take Down Fossil Fuels
item.description:
This is one of a series of potential tactics to use against fossil fuel companies, meant to limit their ability to supply fossil fuels.

1. Phishing for Leaks | 2. Crashing the Roadshow | 3. Amplifying Corporate Dissent (you are here)

This campaign is presented in draft form. It would be helpful …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: June-July Update
item.description:
📢 June - July Update

It's the end of July, and time for an update on my activist strategy and activities. Because this is only for own audience (distribution via email and the website), it's much rougher than other pieces I may put online.

I'm curious to know what you notice about …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: 6 Ways That Pandemics and the Environment Are Inseparable
item.description: Pandemics, ecology, and climate are hopelessly intertwined. The climate and ecological crisis sickens and displaces both wild animals and human beings. It creates excellent conditions for pests like ticks and mosquitoes to breed as well as for deadly bacteria to multiply. And it literally thaws ancient pathogens from the ground …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Why a blog?
item.description:
I'm departing on an activist adventure.

I've worked on the fringes of environmental activism for years. Among my projects, I started a well-reputed Extinction Rebellion group, and worked with friends to map inconsistencies methane leak reporting.

Many of the skills that I've acquired through work and my personal life (agile …
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Shipping Vessel Prediction
item.description: Predicted cargo vessels' next ports of call and arrival times.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Transcript Transcription
item.description: A project to extract structured data from messy university transcript images.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Auto Tuning Models (ATM)
item.description: A scalable application for selecting and tuning machine learning models.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Trane
item.description: A library for automatically generating prediction problems from structured data.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
item.description: Worked on research and management projects at MIT's CSAIL Big Data Initiative and Living Lab.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Industry Outlook
item.description: A data science project analyzing automation across industry sectors.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Robot Studio
item.description: A web app allowing end-users to train ML models without labeled data.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Aurum
item.description: An application for large-scale data discovery with query algebra.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: LostLeaks
item.description: Analyzed natural gas leaks in Eastern Massachusetts to identify anomalies.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: BigDawg
item.description: A data polystore and query system for searching across multiple database types.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: DataHub
item.description: A data collaboration and sharing ecosystem developed at MIT.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: GetFit
item.description: An iOS app for motion data collection in a fitness competition.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: OpenPDS
item.description: A distributed system to preserve user privacy while collecting and sharing data.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Northeastern University
item.description: Earned a Master of Science in Information Systems while working full-time at MIT.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: OpenSensing
item.description: An open-source library for collecting sensor data from iOS devices.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: BikeTrace
item.description: An iOS application for recording and displaying bicycle trips.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Open Source Soylent
item.description: An open-source project to create a meal replacement formula.
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter

item.title: Brown University
item.description: Earned a BA in Commerce, Organizations, and Entrepreneurship (now known as BEO).
item.dc:creator: Albert R Carter
